articles.title: Universal Kids Resort Grand Opens in Frisco, Texas
articles.summary: Universal Destinations & Experiences has officially opened Universal Kids Resort in Frisco, Texas, its first regional theme park designed specifically for children ages 3–8. The park's opening marks a significant expansion of the family entertainment market in the Dallas-Fort Worth area and signals a growing trend of major operators targeting younger demographics with dedicated facilities. FEC operators should monitor how this new competitor affects regional family spending patterns.

articles.title: Holiday World Announces $22M Record-Breaking Water Coaster for 2027
articles.summary: Holiday World in Santa Claus, Indiana has revealed Cannonball, a $22 million water coaster set to open in May 2027, representing the park's largest single-attraction investment ever. Designed by Mack Rides, it will be the fastest water coaster in the US with the nation's tallest drop. The scale of investment underscores how regional amusement parks are doubling down on headline attractions to drive destination traffic, a trend FEC operators should watch as competition for leisure spending intensifies.

articles.title: Holiday World's $22M 'Cannonball' Water Coaster Is the Park's Biggest Investment Ever
articles.summary: Holiday World's newly announced Cannonball water roller coaster will send guests 90 feet into the air before speeding through over 1,000 feet of track and splashing down, making it the largest single-attraction investment in the park's history. The $22 million price tag reflects the broader industry push toward record-breaking, experience-driven attractions that justify destination travel. For FEC operators, this signals rising consumer expectations for wow-factor experiences even at regional venues.

articles.title: Mall of America Launches 'Bubble Planet' Immersive Experience from Exhibition Hub and Merlin
articles.summary: Mall of America has opened Bubble Planet, a 60-to-90-minute immersive attraction co-produced by Exhibition Hub and Merlin Entertainments, featuring themed rooms with giant bubbles, lasers, LED spaces, infinity rooms, and VR experiences. The partnership demonstrates the growing appetite for turnkey immersive experiences placed within retail and entertainment destinations. FEC operators should note how multi-sensory, technology-enhanced walk-through attractions are being used to drive repeat visitation and extend dwell time in mixed-use venues.

articles.title: Six Flags Great Adventure's Project Purple Completes Track Installation Ahead of Opening
articles.summary: Six Flags Great Adventure has reached a major construction milestone on Project Purple, with crews completing the full track structure just hours after the park opened its new Shoreline Pier area. The back-to-back announcements reflect an aggressive investment strategy at the New Jersey resort as Six Flags continues to differentiate its parks post-merger. Operators tracking the competitive landscape should note how stacked capital investments are being used to build multi-season momentum.

articles.title: Six Flags Great Adventure Opens Shoreline Pier at The Boardwalk
articles.summary: Six Flags Great Adventure officially opened Shoreline Pier at The Boardwalk with a ceremonial grand opening attended by New Jersey's Lt. Governor and local officials, reinforcing the park's ongoing multi-year reinvestment strategy. The nautical-themed addition expands the park's entertainment and dining footprint within its Boardwalk zone. For FEC operators, themed zone expansions like this illustrate how larger players are creating immersive, cohesive guest environments to extend visit duration and per-cap spending.

articles.title: AI-Powered Interactive Avatar of Theodore Roosevelt Debuts at New Presidential Library
articles.summary: The Theodore Roosevelt Presidential Library in North Dakota has opened with an AI-driven life-size avatar of the 26th president, developed in partnership between AI lab LemonSlice and Microsoft, allowing visitors to have real-time spoken conversations with a digital Roosevelt. The experience is described as fluid and expressive, pointing to a new frontier for interactive guest engagement in attraction design. FEC operators and experience designers should watch this technology closely as AI-powered character interactions become increasingly viable for themed entertainment and edutainment venues.

articles.title: Alterface and Fantawild Form Strategic Partnership for Next-Gen Interactive Attractions
articles.summary: Interactive technology specialist Alterface and Chinese themed entertainment giant Fantawild signed a strategic partnership at IAAPA Expo Asia in Hong Kong to co-develop next-generation interactive attractions. The deal combines Fantawild's end-to-end theme park development capabilities with Alterface's expertise in interactive game design and shooting attractions. For FEC suppliers and operators, this partnership signals accelerating innovation in interactive ride experiences and could produce new off-the-shelf products relevant to FEC-scale venues.

articles.title: ART Engineering's Adventure Coaster Blends Ride Mechanics with Immersive Storytelling
articles.summary: ART Engineering has formally launched its Adventure Coaster concept, already deployed at Legoland Florida and Legoland California as Galacticoaster, featuring synchronized rotation, interactive elements, and immersive themed environments for family riders. The side-by-side four-across vehicle design and controlled rotation offer a differentiated ride experience built on ART's existing Primordial coaster platform. FEC developers and attraction buyers exploring new coaster concepts for family-focused venues should evaluate this product as a scalable, themed option.

articles.title: Six Flags Names Mark Pauls as New Chief Operating Officer
articles.summary: Six Flags has appointed Mark Pauls as its new chief operating officer, a leadership move that could shape operational direction across the company's extensive portfolio of theme parks and FECs. Leadership changes at major operators like Six Flags often signal shifts in strategy around guest experience, capital allocation, and partner relationships. Suppliers and regional operators should monitor any new operational priorities that emerge under the new COO.

articles.title: Evel Knievel Interactive Museum Opens in Downtown Las Vegas
articles.summary: The Evel Knievel Experience has opened in Las Vegas' Arts District, offering an interactive museum with historic artifacts, 4D virtual reality, and immersive experiences celebrating the iconic daredevil's legacy. Originally launched as a museum in Topeka, Kansas, the concept has been reimagined for a broader global audience in a high-traffic entertainment district. The Vegas opening illustrates how IP-driven interactive museums can successfully reformat and scale into new markets, a model relevant to FEC operators considering branded experiential concepts.

articles.title: Evel Knievel Experience Opens in Las Vegas with Interactive and 4D Elements
articles.summary: The Evel Knievel Experience has debuted in Downtown Las Vegas, housing the world's largest collection of Knievel artifacts alongside immersive 4D virtual reality experiences and interactive installations. The attraction's evolution from a regional Kansas museum to a high-profile Las Vegas destination demonstrates a viable path for scaling interactive entertainment concepts. FEC operators can draw lessons from this model of combining physical artifacts with digital immersion to create compelling, multi-layered guest experiences.

articles.title: 2026 Water Park Season Promises Record-Breaking Attractions and Major Expansions Globally
articles.summary: A new analysis of the 2026 water park landscape reveals that while North America is relatively quiet on single new-attraction additions, several parks are undertaking large-scale expansions and a wave of significant international projects is underway. The trend is away from standard off-the-shelf slides toward record-breaking, themed installations that function more like high-speed attractions. FEC operators with water park components or considering aquatic expansions should note the rising bar for differentiation and the premium being placed on experience over volume.

articles.title: Patrick Mahomes Family Previews Universal Kids Resort, Boosting Pre-Opening Buzz
articles.summary: NFL star Patrick Mahomes and his family visited Universal Kids Resort in Frisco, Texas for a celebrity preview ahead of the park's grand opening, generating significant media attention for the new family-focused venue. The celebrity tie-in strategy highlights how major attraction operators are leveraging influencer and sports star partnerships to build awareness. FEC marketers can take note of how aspirational family content and celebrity endorsement can amplify a venue launch.

articles.title: New Six Flags Fiesta Texas Leadership Outlines Park Strategy
articles.summary: The new head of Six Flags Fiesta Texas has revealed plans for the San Antonio park, signaling a fresh strategic direction for one of the chain's key regional properties. Leadership transitions at major regional parks can reshape competitive dynamics and investment priorities that affect nearby FEC operators. Stakeholders in the Texas entertainment market should follow how the new strategy unfolds.

articles.title: Exhibition Hub and Fever Bring 'Titanic: An Immersive Voyage' to Scottsdale, Arizona
articles.summary: Exhibition Hub and Fever have launched Titanic: An Immersive Voyage in Scottsdale, Arizona, expanding the touring exhibition's North American footprint with an experience that combines projection mapping, life-size recreations, VR, and over 300 historical artifacts. The timing of the launch coincides with the anniversary of the 1912 British inquiry into the disaster, adding cultural relevance to the marketing. The continued rollout of large-scale touring immersive experiences in retail and entertainment venues is a trend FEC operators should monitor as potential partnership or competition in their markets.

articles.title: Six Flags Over Texas Marks America's 250th with Fireworks, Extended Hours, and Special Events
articles.summary: Six Flags Over Texas celebrated the nation's 250th anniversary with its Star-Spangled Nights event, featuring fireworks, live entertainment, limited-time food offerings, and extended park hours over July 3–4. The multi-element holiday programming demonstrates how larger parks are packaging patriotic occasions into full guest experience campaigns. FEC operators can draw inspiration from this approach to holiday theming and special event programming to boost off-peak revenue.

articles.title: Six Flags New England Combines Summer Heat Relief and Fourth of July Fireworks
articles.summary: Six Flags New England promoted its Star Spangled Nights event as a dual-purpose offering, giving families a cool daytime destination during a heat wave while delivering fireworks and patriotic entertainment in the evening. The park's messaging strategically tied weather conditions to an urgency-driven visit proposition. FEC operators can learn from this example of reactive, real-time marketing that turns environmental factors like heat waves into foot traffic drivers.

articles.title: Hersheypark Adds Fireworks to July 4th Weekend for the First Time
articles.summary: Hersheypark introduced fireworks displays for the first time on July 3 and 4 as part of its America's 250th anniversary programming, adding a new evening entertainment layer to its holiday weekend offering. The move reflects how established parks are elevating holiday programming to compete for discretionary leisure spending during peak periods. FEC operators looking to boost holiday visitation should consider how signature evening events like fireworks can extend guest visits and justify premium ticket pricing.

articles.title: Greater Sentosa Master Plan Outlines Major New Attractions from Early 2030s
articles.summary: Singapore's Greater Sentosa Master Plan has confirmed that new major attractions will begin opening from the early 2030s, signaling one of the most ambitious leisure destination development programs in Asia. The plan positions Sentosa as a global benchmark for integrated resort and FEC-style development at scale. International operators and investors tracking long-horizon opportunities in Asian leisure markets should note the scale and ambition of this project.

articles.title: Sphere Las Vegas Debuts America's 250th Birthday Display
articles.summary: Sphere in Las Vegas launched a special 'Nation of Wonder' exterior display to mark America's 250th anniversary, continuing its use of the massive LED exterior as a dynamic branding and cultural engagement canvas. The activation reinforces how landmark venue technology can serve dual purposes as both attraction and media platform. For FEC operators exploring experiential marketing, Sphere's content-driven exterior activations offer a compelling case study in using physical assets for event-tied audience engagement.

articles.title: Six Flags Over Texas Sets New Opening Date for Tormenta Rampaging Run
articles.summary: Six Flags Over Texas has announced a revised opening date for its new attraction Tormenta Rampaging Run, keeping consumer anticipation alive while the park manages its construction timeline. Delayed attraction openings remain a common operational challenge for parks balancing safety, construction, and marketing commitments. FEC operators can note the importance of proactive communication strategies when new attractions face schedule changes.

articles.title: Six Flags Great Adventure Opens 'Shoreline Pier at The Boardwalk' Expansion
articles.summary: Six Flags Great Adventure has officially opened Shoreline Pier at The Boardwalk, a new themed area expanding the park's entertainment and dining offerings within its coastal-themed Boardwalk zone. The addition is part of the park's broader multi-year reinvestment strategy to grow per-cap spending and visit duration. FEC operators can observe how strategically themed food and entertainment zones are being used by major parks to create destination-within-a-destination experiences.

articles.title: Universal's New Texas Theme Park: A Guide to What's Inside
articles.summary: A visitor guide to Universal Kids Resort in Frisco, Texas highlights its key attractions including Jurassic World and Minions-themed areas tailored for young children. The park's opening introduces a new format of licensed IP-driven regional family entertainment specifically targeting the under-8 demographic. FEC operators in the Texas region should assess how this new destination may shift family entertainment spending patterns in the coming season.

articles.title: Amusement Today July 2026 Issue Now Available
articles.summary: The July 2026 edition of Amusement Today is now available, covering the latest news, openings, and industry developments across the amusement and FEC sector. The monthly publication is a key resource for operators, suppliers, and investors tracking trends and business developments. FEC professionals should check this issue for in-depth coverage of current industry events.

articles.title: British Museum's Bayeux Tapestry Exhibition Sells Out in Record-Breaking Demand
articles.summary: The British Museum generated over £2.5 million in ticket sales in a single day for its upcoming Bayeux Tapestry exhibition, with over 65,000 people queuing online and initial availability quickly selling out. The extraordinary demand demonstrates the commercial power of blockbuster cultural exhibitions when paired with strong IP and strategic ticketing. FEC operators offering ticketed experiences can draw lessons from the museum's urgency-driven release strategy and phased ticket availability approach.

articles.title: Bompas & Parr Brings Interactive Honey and Finance Exhibition to Bank of England Museum
articles.summary: Experiential design studio Bompas & Parr has created an interactive exhibition at the Bank of England Museum featuring live bees, scent challenges, UV investigations, and family trails exploring themes of trust and deception. The project is a strong example of how experiential designers are transforming traditional museum spaces into multi-sensory, participatory environments. FEC operators and experience designers can look to this model as inspiration for layering interactive and educational content into family-friendly attractions.
